title: Question for Jackson — graph-theory / discrete-math study questions
html:
TL;DR

  • June 2022 list of study questions for "jackson" — entirely discrete math / graph theory topics from OSU sophomore math (likely MTH 231 or CS 225 equivalent).
  • Topics: weighted-vs-multigraph adjacency-matrix representation, the binomial theorem, the handshake theorem, bipartite graphs, "Study Hall's theorem" (likely Hall's marriage theorem), Ore's / Dirac's theorems for Hamiltonian cycles, and incidence matrices.
  • Confirms jackson as a discrete-math study buddy in spring/summer 2022 — important because jackson also appears in late-2022 OSIG / coding contexts.
  • The "Ed multiple edges" typo ("how do we represent Ed multiple edges in a matrix") is a giveaway for voice-dictation capture.
  • Functional artifact: this is a rare math-coursework deep capture — most 2022 coursework notes are admin-only ("do MTH quiz"), not topic-level.

Why it matters

Establishes the content depth of Yahir's spring-2022 coursework — he was actually wrestling with non-trivial graph-theory results, not just executing assignments. Worth noting the conceptual overlap with the graph-as-data-structure vault metaphor that animates this whole secondbrain vault (the CLAUDE.md "maximize connection density of the graph") — Yahir was already thinking in adjacency matrices and bipartite graphs at the same time as he was writing the dream-fragment notes.

Open questions

  • Who is jackson specifically? (Recurs in Sept 2022 OSIG note and 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream — likely a CS classmate / OSIG analyst.)
  • Did Yahir actually take a Hamiltonian-cycles class, or was Hall's/Ore's/Dirac's coming from one chapter of the discrete-math text?
  • Is there a connection between this graph-theory grounding and Yahir's later instinct to "store everything in a graph" via obsidian? (Tempting but speculative.)

Wikilinks

jackson · discrete math · graph theory · binomial theorem · handshake theorem · bipartite graphs · osu · 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream
